nodes.label: React Router
nodes.rationale: React Router is mode-specific; identify the mode before changing an app to avoid applying the wrong patterns.

nodes.label: Framework Mode
nodes.rationale: Full-stack mode using @react-router/dev Vite plugin, app/routes.ts, route modules, SSR/SPA/pre-rendering.

nodes.label: Data Mode
nodes.rationale: Uses data routers (createBrowserRouter, etc.) and RouterProvider without the Framework Vite plugin or route-module file conventions.

nodes.label: Declarative Mode
nodes.rationale: Simplest mode using BrowserRouter/Routes/Route JSX components; no loaders, actions, or data-router APIs.

nodes.label: RSC Framework Mode
nodes.rationale: Framework Mode with unstable RSC Vite plugin (unstable_reactRouterRSC, @vitejs/plugin-rsc).

nodes.label: RSC Data Mode
nodes.rationale: Data Mode with low-level RSC runtime APIs and unstable_RSCRouteConfig.

nodes.label: Mode Migration
nodes.rationale: Do not apply Framework/Data patterns to a Declarative app unless intentionally migrating modes.

nodes.label: Framework Mode (Full-Stack)
nodes.rationale: Full-stack mode with SSR, SPA, pre-rendering; uses Vite plugin, route modules, generated types.

nodes.label: Generated Route Type Safety
nodes.rationale: Import types from ./+types/<route>; use Route.LoaderArgs, Route.ActionArgs, Route.ComponentProps; do not edit generated .react-router/types files.

nodes.label: Rendering Strategy
nodes.rationale: Framework Mode supports SSR, SPA, pre-rendered, or mixed rendering strategies depending on config and route behavior.

nodes.label: Data Mode (Data Routers)
nodes.rationale: Uses data routers (createBrowserRouter, createHashRouter, createMemoryRouter) and RouterProvider. SSR is manual and lower-level than Framework Mode.

nodes.label: Data Mode SSR
nodes.rationale: Data Mode SSR is manual and lower-level than Framework Mode. Uses createStaticHandler, createStaticRouter, StaticRouterProvider.

nodes.label: Declarative Mode (JSX)
nodes.rationale: Uses BrowserRouter, Routes, Route JSX components. Simplest mode with no loaders, actions, or data APIs.

nodes.label: Declarative Mode Boundary
nodes.rationale: Declarative Mode lacks loaders, actions, Form, useFetcher, useNavigation, route module exports. Recommend Data/Framework migration for data-driven needs.

nodes.label: React Server Components (RSC)
nodes.rationale: RSC support is explicitly unstable. Check installed versions before implementing or refactoring.

nodes.label: RSC Framework Mode
nodes.rationale: Framework Mode with unstable RSC Vite plugin (reactRouterRSC + rsc plugins). Routes can use server component exports.

nodes.label: RSC Data Mode
nodes.rationale: More manual than RSC Framework Mode. Uses unstable_RSCRouteConfig and low-level RSC server APIs.

nodes.label: Client/Server Boundary
nodes.rationale: Must respect React's client/server split: use 'use client' for hooks/browser APIs, server-only for data access, and client-only for browser-specific code.

nodes.label: RSC Data Loading
nodes.rationale: RSC changes where data can be loaded: Server Components can fetch directly; loaders/actions may still exist with RSC-specific behavior; client components cannot access server-only modules.

nodes.label: FLOW FORGE wordmark text in custom typeface
Pinwheel/flow icon made of four circles in infinite-loop pattern with red connecting paths
Dark theme logo variant with white text on dark background
Light theme logo variant with dark text on light background
